title: Intmax
description: A stateless zkRollup (L3) optimised for private payments, pushing most data off-chain so that on-chain costs are minimal while preserving user-side privacy through client-side proving.

properties.name: Confidentiality
properties.notes: Users construct their own transaction batches locally. These batches consist of arbitrary mappings from recipients to token amounts and are not revealed to aggregators. Each transaction batch is salted and hashed. Only these hashes are submitted to the aggregator, preserving privacy. The transaction data stays in the user device while the transaction hash is sent to the aggregator and later published on-chain.

properties.name: Anonymity
properties.notes: Sender and receiver are hidden when a transaction is submitted to the network. When a sender wants to send a transaction they need to compute a ZK proof showing they have enough balance. The ZK proof and the transaction details are sent to the receiver so they can update their balance in their local state (end-user device). The transaction hashes are sent to the aggregator to publish the newest state on-chain.

properties.name: Asset privacy
properties.notes: When users deposit assets into or withdraw assets from the Intmax network the token type is recorded on Ethereum L1. There is no asset privacy when entering or leaving the protocol. On the network itself, the private transfers do not reveal the asset being transmitted, only salted hashes of the transactions are published on-chain.

properties.name: Plausible deniability
properties.notes: Deposits from Ethereum to Intmax are submitted as standard L1 transactions to the Intmax Rollup contract, making it publicly visible on-chain that a user is depositing into the Intmax L3. Internal private transfers could be plausible denied because only salted hashes of the transactions are submitted. No transaction details like sender, recipient, amount and transfer metadata get published on the network.

properties.name: Number of secrets
properties.notes: The Intmax private key is generated by the connected Ethereum wallet — it is derived, not independently created. Users only need to store their Ethereum wallet key. The Intmax key can be exported for offline backup.

properties.name: Time-to-finality
properties.notes: Intmax is an L3 settling on Scroll (L2) which settles on Ethereum (L1). Funds are stored on Ethereum. Finality depends on how long it takes for validity proofs to be submitted and verified. Scroll's finality time (~43 minutes as of writing) is the primary bottleneck, as Intmax blocks are submitted to Scroll. TODO: verify current Intmax-specific finality time — it should be at least Scroll's finality, possibly longer if Intmax block builder submission adds delay

properties.name: Deposit time
properties.notes: It takes about 30 minutes for a Ethereum L1 to Intmax deposit to be reflected. This is related to the time it takes the block builders to publish the new state root with the new private balance of the user.

properties.name: Withdraw time
properties.notes: It takes about 6 hours for an Intmax to Ethereum L1 withdrawal transaction to be reflected. This is related to the withdrawal server or withdrawal aggregator time it takes to verify the withdrawal zk proof, post the data on-chain (waiting for hard finality) and transferring funds to the public recipients.

properties.name: Censorship resistance
properties.notes: There is no central operator — sequencing and block production are permissionless. Users can force transactions to be included by sending them to L1, and proposing new blocks requires creating ZK proofs. However, deposits are gated by Predicate AVS for AML compliance, which can block deposits from flagged addresses. The Liquidity contract operator can also pause deposits and withdrawals.

properties.name: External network dependence
properties.notes: Intmax relies on a network of external nodes (block builders, aggregators, and validity provers) to process and settle transactions. These roles are permissionless, meaning anyone can run the required services and participate in the network without needing approval from the core team.

properties.name: Escape hatch
properties.value: Can exit in a time period
properties.notes: Users can autonomously exit by providing a ZK proof of sufficient balance to the L1 bridge on Ethereum. This requires keeping track of all funds received and sent. If the proposer fails, users can leverage the source-available prover to submit proofs directly. However, there is no exit window for unwanted upgrades since contracts are instantly upgradable.

properties.name: Upgradeability
properties.notes: Intmax contracts on Scroll use the EIP1967 proxy pattern with a 2/4 threshold multisig admin, enabling upgrades with no delay. There is no exit window for users in case of unwanted upgrades — contracts are instantly upgradable. The ITX token exists for privacy mining rewards but no governance functionality has been documented.

properties.name: Client-side proving
properties.notes: ZK proofs for Intmax transactions are generated on the user's device in supported web and desktop wallet applications. No external prover service has access to private transaction data. Aggregators only receive salted transaction hashes.

properties.name: Third-party inspectability
properties.notes: Transaction contents are end-to-end encrypted and proven with ZK proofs. Neither the network operators nor external observers can read private transfer data. All balance data is self-custodied by users — funds can be lost if users lose this data.

properties.name: Implementation maturity
properties.notes: Intmax launched on Ethereum mainnet in June 2025

properties.name: Post-quantum secure
properties.notes: Intmax uses the Plonky2 proof system with BN254 elliptic curve pairings for Ethereum compatibility, Keccak (SHA-3) for ZK-friendly hashing, and 32-bit integer arithmetic in its circuits. The SNARK construction requires a trusted setup. While Plonky2's inner proof uses hash-based FRI commitments (potentially post-quantum resistant), the reliance on BN254 for pairing operations makes the system vulnerable to quantum attacks via Shor's algorithm.

properties.name: Layer of enforcement
properties.notes: AML compliance is enforced at the deposit stage through Predicate AVS, which screens incoming transactions. The network blocks deposits from high-risk addresses and requires compliance approval before funds can enter the shielded pool.

properties.name: Enforcement entities
properties.notes: Compliance screening is performed by Predicate AVS, a third-party AML service that gates deposits into the Intmax network.

properties.name: Type of compliance
properties.notes: Intmax gates incoming deposits via Predicate AVS — a programmable per-deposit policy framework that returns a cryptographic attestation of compliance against rule sets sourcing from blockchain-analytics vendors. Predicate is the canonical example of programmatic policies. Users additionally demonstrate via a Proof of Innocence model that their funds are not associated with sanctioned or illicit activity before being accepted into the network.

properties.name: Point of enforcement
properties.notes: AML and compliance checks are applied exclusively at the deposit stage when users bridge assets from Ethereum L1 into the Intmax network. Once funds are inside the network, subsequent transfers are not subject to additional compliance screening. Withdrawals are assumed to be clean if no bad actor has deposited into the network.

properties.name: Selective disclosure: viewing entity
properties.notes: Intmax has no viewing key or protocol-level selective disclosure mechanism. Users can manually share individual transaction details, ZK proofs, and on-chain references with third parties, but there is no systematic viewing access.

properties.name: Selective disclosure: viewing control
properties.notes: There is no viewing key system or programmable disclosure control. Users can manually share specific transaction proofs but this is ad-hoc, not a protocol feature.

properties.name: Cryptographic verifiability
properties.notes: State transitions within Intmax are cryptographically verified using Plonky2 ZK proofs with FRI commitments and BN254 curve operations — an external observer can re-check the proofs against the published verifier. Additional trust concerns (contracts upgradable by a 2/4 multisig, Predicate AVS deposit gating, self-custodied balance data) are captured separately by Upgradeability and Censorship resistance rather than this property. The system settles through Scroll (L2) to Ethereum (L1), inheriting their consensus models for ordering.

properties.name: Open source
properties.notes: The Intmax smart contracts, node software, and related tooling are publicly available on GitHub under the InternetMaximalism organization. TODO: verify license type — no LICENSE file found in the main repositories as of April 2026

properties.name: Private State Scalability
properties.notes: Users maintain their own state locally on their devices. A significant part of state growth is distributed across individual users. Aggregators post the Merkle root, aggregated signature, and public key list to the rollup contract. The contract verifies aggregate proofs

properties.name: Client-side indexing
properties.notes: Transactions are shared peer-to-peer from the sender to the recipient. The recipient needs to verify a ZK proof in order to update its own client-side state. Only salted hashes of the transactions are published on-chain in order to help receivers get track of the global state and the unused notes (UTXOS).

properties.name: Private state model
properties.notes: Intmax represents private balances as a set of UTXOs. Each received transfer creates a new unspent output controlled by the recipient, and spending requires consuming existing UTXOs and creating new ones.

properties.name: Private Data Storage
properties.notes: Intmax minimises on-chain footprint by storing only block roots and validity proofs on Ethereum L1. All transaction and balance data is self-custodied by users, who must maintain balance proofs received from aggregators during deposits and transfers. On-chain commitments serve as the source of truth for finality.

properties.name: Access to DeFi
properties.notes: Intmax is designed primarily for private payments and asset transfers. Users must withdraw to Ethereum L1 to access the broader DeFi ecosystem. While the network supports applications for gaming and NFTs, there is no native DeFi infrastructure.

properties.name: Programmability / Generality
properties.notes: Intmax provides an SDK for developers to build applications on the network. While the primary use case is private payments, the architecture supports token transfers, NFTs, and basic application logic. Arbitrary smart contract execution is not supported.
